name: the-forgotten-highlander-an-incredible-wwii-story-of-survival-in-the-pacific
description: Alistair Urquhart's The Forgotten Highlander: An Incredible WWII Story of Survival in the Pacific — a war memoir and survival psychology toolkit chronicling one man's unimaginable ordeal: capture by the Japanese in Singapore, 750 days as a slave on the Death Railway, torpedoing of the hellship Kachidoki Maru, five days adrift alone at sea, and the atomic bombing of Nagasaki.
useCase: Understand the forgotten Pacific War — the Death Railway, hellships, and the Asian Holocaust — through one survivor's extraordinary story of survival against impossible odds.
knownRisks: The book describes extreme violence, torture, and suffering — may be deeply distressing
Urquhart's account is from memory 60+ years later — some details may be imprecise
His anger toward Japan may feel one-sided; he does not attempt balanced historical analysis
